sections.paragraph:
κουμ (mss. and edd. also κούμ, κοῦμ, κουμί) Mesopotamian form of the impv. קוּם, for which Palestinian Aramaic has קוּמִי stand up Mk 5:41 (Wlh. ad loc.).—TRE III 609.

edd. edd. = editions, editors

impv. impv. = imperative

Wlh. Wlh. = JWellhausen, Einleitung—List 6

ad loc. ad loc. = ad locum (to or at the place under consideration)

TRE TRE = Theologische Realenzyklopädie—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 563
